title: 1.1: Tests that file upload to root directory succeeds when user has full permissions on root
1.2: Tests that file upload to subdirectory fails when subdirectory lacks upload permission
1.3: Tests that new file upload fails with only overwrite permission (no upload permission)
1.4: Tests that overwriting an existing file succeeds with overwrite permission
1.5: Tests that file download fails when subdirectory lacks download permission
1.6: Tests that chtimes operation on subdirectory directory fails when parent directory lacks chtimes permission
1.7: Tests that chtimes operation on file within subdirectory succeeds when subdirectory has chtimes permission
1.8: Tests that readdir on subdirectory fails when subdirectory lacks list permission
1.9: Tests that mkdir in subdirectory fails when subdirectory lacks create directory permission
1.10: Tests that rmdir in subdirectory fails when subdirectory lacks delete permission
1.11: Tests that stat operation on subdirectory succeeds when user has any permission on that path
1.12: Tests that rename into subdirectory fails when subdirectory lacks rename permission
1.13: Tests that symlink into subdirectory fails when subdirectory lacks symlink permission
2.1: Places the test for upload operations in the sftpd directory
2.2: Places the test for overwrite operations in the sftpd directory
2.3: Places the test for download operations in the sftpd directory
2.4: Places the test for setstat operations in the sftpd directory
2.5: Places the test for directory commands in the sftpd directory
3.1: All tests use the Go testing framework with *testing.T parameter
3.2: All tests follow the TestCamelCase naming pattern
3.3: All tests report failures using t.Errorf, t.Error, t.Fatal, or t.Fatalf
3.4: All tests clean up created resources after test completion
4.1: Tests configure different permissions for root vs subdirectory paths
4.2: Tests use the dataprovider permission constants
4.3: Tests establish SFTP client connections for testing
